name: Obon
traditions: Bon Odori folk dances; floating paper lanterns (toro nagashi); visiting family graves; making food offerings; lighting mukaebi welcome fires.
historicalBackground: Obon originated from the Buddhist Ullambana Sutra. It evolved in Japan over 500 years, blending Buddhist ancestor veneration with native Shinto practices.
description: A Japanese Buddhist festival honoring the spirits of deceased ancestors, celebrated with traditional dances and floating lantern ceremonies.
